dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a 92-year-old female with stroke-like symptoms and a call type of sick person.
Ma'am, I'm EMT Alex, and this is EMT Jordan with the rescue squad. Can you tell us what happened today?
She started showing left-side weakness while I was bathing her. She became unresponsive.
Ma'am, can you open your eyes or nod your head if you understand me?
She's opening her eyes to verbal stimulus and seems aware.
Can you tell me your name or point to your nose?
If you can squeeze my fingers, do so now. She squeezed once, weak on the left side.
She is not showing signs of airway obstruction.
She's in a Fowler's position. That helps maintain airway patency.
Patent, maintainable; EMT: Airway is clear and patent. No intervention is needed right now.

dialogue.role: Rate and depth of respirations; Partner
dialogue.utterance: Respirations are initially at 13, and not labored.

dialogue.role: Oxygen saturation measured with pulse oximetry; Partner
dialogue.utterance: Oxygen saturation is 96% via nasal cannula, no hypoxia noted at first.

dialogue.role: Presence of bilateral breath sounds; EMT
dialogue.utterance: Clear, equal breath sounds bilaterally-no wheezes or crackles on our first check.
Pulse is 52, weak, and irregularly irregular on our initial assessment.
Peripheral pulses are weak, slow, and irregular in all extremities.
Blood pressure is 120/57, stable for now.
Skin is warm, pink, and dry-no signs of pallor.
Glasgow Coma Score is 11-Eye 3, Verbal 2, Motor 6.
Are there any other symptoms or changes from baseline we should know about?
She has a history of atrial fibrillation-no prior stroke reported.
She takes Xarelto for A-Fib.
She's allergic to Codeine and Promethazine.
She was last seen normal at 08:15 AM.
Ma'am, on a scale of 1 to 10, do you have any pain?

dialogue.role: Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils; EMT
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are PERRL-equal, round, reactive to light.
No neck stiffness or pain observed.
No JVD noted.
Nose is clear, mouth is open, no drainage or odors.
Thorax rises equally with no deformity.
Lung sounds are clear in all fields, no crackles or wheezes noted.
Abdomen is not assessed per standard practice for this case.
Weakness noted on left side-no deformities or abrasions on examination.
No rashes, petechiae, purpura, or birthmarks noted. DCAP-BTLS findings are absent.
Blood glucose test is administered; reading is zero.
Let's apply 4-lead ECG for continuous cardiac monitoring.
Jordan, let's start an IV in the left arm to maintain access.
We'll follow the medical stroke/TIA protocol-field impression is possible CVA.
Blood pressure increased to 144/79-stable, no signs of acute change observed.
Respirations at 16, breathing stable and unlabored.
Pulse is 39, still weak and irregularly irregular.
SpO2 dropped to 92%-will check again in a few minutes.
12-lead ECG performed and transmitted to the receiving hospital.
Patient is secured on the stretcher with 5-point harness prior to loading.
All straps are tightened-patient monitor attached and active.
Transport to [COMPANY_NAME] initiated-EDM advised for CT imaging immediately.
Blood glucose still reads zero-documented and monitored regularly.
No new symptoms identified-weakness persistent on left.
She remains without any pain complaints.
Breathing rate decreased to 15 but regular.
Final blood pressure is 164/145-hemodynamically stable now.
Final pulse resting at 50-still stable but bradycardic.
EMS care transferred to ED nurse upon arrival at the hospital.
Completed field care sheet and transferred to hospital ASAP.
Patient on CT scanner bed with protective restraints in place.
Confirmed atrial fibrillation, no further changes observed in telemetry.
Paperwork completed-handoff to ED nurse finalized at 10:05.
All equipment disconnected and secured in ambulance pre-pack.
ED nurse verbally confirmed patient acceptance and condition on arrival.
Remote ECG monitoring completed with close observation.
No symptoms worsened with movement or interaction.
Hospital confirmed-no delays in emergent arrival.
Paperwork and equipment submitted and cleaned for next call.
SpO2 improved to 99%-a good response noted.
EMT Alex and Jordan aside while ED nurse completes hospital intake.
Final blood pressure recorded at 130/51-pulse remains stable.
No medication administered during transport per medical control orders.
ED nurse confirmed readiness for further care at [COMPANY_NAME].
No pain complained throughout the entire assessment and transport.
Final vital signs documented for final report and hospital handoff.
Final check complete-patient care report was unable to be printed due to high call volume.
